an1.11:1.1: “Bhikkhus, I do not see any other single thing that, when not yet arisen, gives rise to sensual desire, or that, when arisen, causes sensual desire to grow and spread, as does the sign of beauty.
an1.11:1.2: Bhikkhus, when one gives improper attention to the sign of beauty, sensual desire, not yet arisen, arises, and sensual desire, already arisen, causes growth and expansion”.
an1.12:1.1: “Bhikkhus, I do not see any other single thing that, when not yet arisen, gives rise to ill will, or that, when arisen, causes ill will to grow and spread, as does the sign of resistance.
an1.12:1.2: Bhikkhus, when one gives improper attention to the sign of resistance, ill will, not yet arisen, arises, and ill will, already arisen, causes growth and expansion”.
an1.13:1.1: “Bhikkhus, I do not see any other single thing that, when not yet arisen, gives rise to sloth and torpor, or that, when arisen, causes sloth and torpor to grow and spread, as do discontent, sluggishness, yawning, drowsiness after a meal, and the mind’s sluggishness.
an1.13:1.2: Bhikkhus, for one whose mind is sluggish, sloth and torpor, not yet arisen, arise, and sloth and torpor, already arisen, cause growth and expansion”.
an1.14:1.1: “Bhikkhus, I do not see any other single thing that, when not yet arisen, gives rise to restlessness and remorse, or that, when arisen, causes restlessness and remorse to grow and spread, as does the mind’s lack of calm.
an1.14:1.2: Bhikkhus, for one whose mind is not calmed, restlessness and remorse, not yet arisen, arise, and restlessness and remorse, already arisen, cause growth and expansion”.
an1.15:1.1: “Bhikkhus, I do not see any other single thing that, when not yet arisen, gives rise to doubt, or that, when arisen, causes doubt to grow and spread, as does improper attention.
an1.15:1.2: Bhikkhus, when one gives improper attention, doubt, not yet arisen, arises, and doubt, already arisen, causes growth and expansion”.
an1.16:1.1: “Bhikkhus, I do not see any other single thing that, when not yet arisen, gives rise to sensual desire, or that, when arisen, causes sensual desire to cease, as does the sign of the unattractive.
an1.16:1.2: Bhikkhus, when one gives appropriate attention to the sign of the unattractive, sensual desire, not yet arisen, does not arise, and sensual desire, already arisen, ceases”.
an1.17:1.1: “Bhikkhus, I do not see any other single thing that, when not yet arisen, gives rise to ill will, or that, when arisen, causes ill will to cease, as does the liberation of mind through goodwill.
an1.17:1.2: Bhikkhus, when one gives appropriate attention to the liberation of mind through goodwill, ill will, not yet arisen, does not arise, and ill will, already arisen, ceases”.
an1.18:1.1: “Bhikkhus, I do not see any other single thing that, when not yet arisen, gives rise to sloth and torpor, or that, when arisen, causes sloth and torpor to cease, as do the element of arousing, the element of striving, and the element of perseverance.
an1.18:1.2: Bhikkhus, for one whose energy is aroused, sloth and torpor, not yet arisen, do not arise, and sloth and torpor, already arisen, cease”.
an1.19:1.1: “Bhikkhus, I do not see any other single thing that, when not yet arisen, gives rise to restlessness and remorse, or that, when arisen, causes restlessness and remorse to cease, as does the mind’s calm.
an1.19:1.2: Bhikkhus, for one whose mind is calmed, restlessness and remorse, not yet arisen, do not arise, and restlessness and remorse, already arisen, cease”.
an1.20:1.1: “Bhikkhus, I do not see any other single thing that, when not yet arisen, gives rise to doubt, or that, when arisen, causes doubt to cease, as does appropriate attention.
an1.20:1.2: Bhikkhus, when one gives appropriate attention, doubt, not yet arisen, does not arise, and doubt, already arisen, ceases”.
an1.20:1.4: The chapter on abandoning hindrances, the second.
